*** 2 INJ *** EF-1 TORNADO CONFIRMED BY STORM SURVEY TEAM. TOUCHDOWN WAS NEAR SIBLEY ROAD AND TRACK CONTINUED NORTHWEST FOR APRROXIMATELY 1 MILE.THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED NEAR JOEL MCDONALD ROAD WHERE ONE MOBILE HOME WAS FLIPPED AND ANOTHER MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED.AT LEAST 1 DOZEN HARDWOOD TREES WERE UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH WHICH HAD A WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. THE DAMAGE PATH CONTINUED TO AND ENDED NEAR MILLER LANE AT WOODHAVEN ROAD. PRELIMINARY ESTIMATE OF PEAK WIND SPEED IS 110 MPH.

*** 1 INJ *** ELDERLY WOMAN INJURED WHEN HER MOBILE HOME WAS SHIFTED FROM ITS FOUNDATION ON KENNEDY ST. ALSO MINOR DAMAGE TO TREES AND AN APARTMNENT COMPLEX CAR PORT. A WEAK TORNADO CROSSED BAYOU DULARGE AND HIGHWAY 315 ABOUT 8.9 MILES SOUTHWEST OF THE COMMUNITY OF THERIOT. MINOR ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED TO A FEW HOMES AND SHEET METAL ROOF PANELS WERE PULLED FROM ROOFS OF STORAGE BUILDINGS. FOUR MEDIUM SIZED BOATS WERE OVERTURNED IN BAYOU DULARGE. ISOLATED WIND DAMAGE WAS NOTED IN AN AREA FROM APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES WEST OF MCNEIL TO JUST NORTHWEST OF THE COMMUNITY OF MCNEIL. DAMAGE CONSISTED PRIMARILY OF A FEW DOWNED LARGE TREE LIMBS. MINOR ROOF DAMAGE WAS ALSO REPORTED TO A MOBILE HOME BY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

NWS DAMAGE SURVEY FOUND TORNADO DAMAGE BEGINNING JUST WEST OF HIGHWAY 53 AND THEN TRACKING EAST-NORTHEAST ACROSS HIGHWAY 53 FOR ABOUT A HALF MILE. MULTIPLE LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE DOWN...A FEW TREES AND WOODEN POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED...AND A MOBILE HOME AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED. BASED ON THESE DAMAGE INDICATORS...THE NWS RATES THIS DAMAGE AS AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT 100 MPH. A PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY HALF MILE AND A PATH WIDTH OF 75 YARDS WAS ALSO DETERMINED.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT 100MPH. THE TORNADO BEGAN NEAR HIGHWAY 190 AND WOODSIDE DRIVE AROUND 253AM AND TRAVELLED NORTHEAST. AN 18 WHEELER WAS FLIPPED AND A TRAILER WAS REPOSITIONED ALONG WITH A METAL ROOF BEING RIPPED OFF A BUILDING. A DOOR TO THE SAME BUILDING WAS BLOWN INWARD AS WELL. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR CANE MARKET ROAD AND BLOUNT LANE AROUND 256AM. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 2.3 MILES AND PATH WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS.
